stories.title: Trump is tired of arming allies. This country is stepping up.
stories.summary: The United States can’t be on the hook anymore for protecting the world, the president lectured. “The defense of freedom is everybody’s business — not just America’s business,” he said in a White House address. “And it is particularly the responsibility of the people whose freedom is threatened.” What feels like a quintessentially Trumpian speech was delivered by Richard Nixon in 1969. Long before Donald Trump was unnerving Europe by scaling back U.S. security commitments to the continent, Nixon

stories.title: From the US-Mexico border to protests in Poland: highlights of PhotoEspaña 2026
stories.summary: Spain’s leading festival of photography showcases the work of more than 300 visual artists in nearly 100 exhibitions across the country PhotoEspaña, Spain’s leading festival of photography, held its official opening in Madrid this month and by September nearly 100 exhibitions will have showcased the work of more than 300 visual artists in the capital and across the country. Loosely corralled under the theme of reimagining, the exhibitions feature work by major figures in Spanish and internationa

stories.title: Condemned to plutocracy? The relentless rise of US inequality
stories.summary: Elon Musk is a beneficiary of America’s lopsided prosperity – does the country have any appetite for redistribution? As Barack Obama’s presidency was coming to a close, Jason Furman, then chairman of the president’s council of economic advisers, laid out the strides his administration had made to curb the nation’s exorbitant income inequality in “the largest investments in reducing inequality since the Great Society”. Indeed, by the end of 2016, taxes and transfers cut the share of income accrui

stories.title: I challenge the Rothko naysayers to stand in front of his monumental art and not feel awe – Rhiannon Lucy Cosslett
stories.summary: An exhibition in Florence that pairs his giant canvases with Renaissance religious art brought me to the edge of tears. It is the perfect refuge from the infinite scroll As an unbaptised agnostic raised with no religion, the closest I ever really come to a spiritual experience is when I’m standing in front of an artwork. Last week I went to Florence to do exactly that, drawn there not by Michelangelo’s David or Botticelli’s Birth of Venus, but by the works of Mark Rothko, that titan of US abstra

stories.title: Zelenskyy returns Poland's highest honor as row deepens
stories.summary: Ukraine's president has returned a medal to Poland after a decision to strip him of the country's highest honor. Warsaw has been one of Kyiv's key allies since Russia began its war in Ukraine.

stories.title: Zelensky returns highest Polish honour after award stripped
stories.summary: Ukraine's president said his country was open to "engagement" about "difficult and painful chapters of our shared past".

stories.title: Jön a pénz, de olyan támogatási rendszer is kell, ami lendületet ad a magyar cégeknek
stories.summary: Ismét lesznek uniós pénzek a vállalati fejlesztések támogatásához, és jó lenne, ha ezek a növekedés, a felzárkózás forrásává válnának, nem pedig csak lehívnánk őket.

stories.title: Írország egyszer véletlenül legalizálta a drogokat
stories.summary: Egy joghézag miatt egy napra 125-féle kábítószer birtoklása és fogyasztása lett legális. A törvényhozók gyorsan reagáltak, az ilyesmire fogékony íreknek viszont volt egy lazább napjuk.

stories.title: Magyar alapítású dróngyártó cég lett a német védelmi ipar egyik legígéretesebb szereplője
stories.summary: Az alig három éve alapított, elhárítódrónokat fejlesztő és gyártó Tytan Technologies Ukrajnába is szállít a drónjaiból, Németország pedig több tízmillió eurós megbízást adott a startupnak, hogy kifejlesszék a német katonai bázisok drónvédelmét.

stories.title: Kötelező drogtesztelést vezetnek be a francia minisztériumokban
stories.summary: Fokozzák a drogellenes háborút, ebben pedig a politikusoknak is részt kell venni.

stories.title: Nem is millimétereken, szó szerint hajszálon múlt a japán gól a vb-n
stories.summary: Sorban a második vb-n van millimétereken múló japán helyzet. Négy éve gól lett, most éppen csak, hogy nem.

stories.title: Havi 3500 forint lesz az elektromos közbicikli Budapesten, leteszteltük az e-Bubit
stories.summary: Július második felétől ennyi pénzért el lehet vinni bármilyen biciklit az új Mol Bubi flottából fél órára, sőt, akár kettőt is. A BKK-bérletesek további 20 százalék engedményt kapnak.

stories.title: 14 évesen azt mondták, nincs jövője, 17 évesen egy gyárban dolgozott, most 33 percenként szállítja a német válogatottban a gólokat
stories.summary: A német szövetségi kapitány egyelőre kitart amellett, hogy szupercsereként veti be a vb-n az Elefántcsontpart elleni meccset is eldöntő Deniz Undavot.

stories.title: Magyar Péter a vagyonvédelmi hivatalról szóló javaslat legerősebb változatának támogatására kéri a Tiszát
stories.summary: A miniszterelnök szerint a társadalmi egyeztetés során is szigorodhat a vagyonvisszaszerzési hivatalról szóló tervezet.

stories.title: Vitézy: A Dunakeszi Járműjavító csődje miatt kellett osztrák vasúti kocsikat bérelnie a MÁV-nak
stories.summary: Itthon is lenne elég klimatizált kocsi, csak a közlekedési miniszter szerint mind a járműjavítóban vannak, amit az Orbán-kormány orosz és neres köröknek privatizált.

stories.title: Even in this age of what Mark Carney calls global rupture, do not despair: there is still hope for international law | Nathalie Tocci
stories.summary: Developments in Ukraine and Iran show that the military superpowers are not getting it all their own way Our age of what Mark Carney called global rupture is also often described as following the “ law of the jungle ”, in which the strong do what they can and the weak suffer what they must, with international law shattered and multilateral organisations hollowed out. Russia’s invasion of Ukraine, Israel’s onslaught on Gaza, and the US and Israel’s attacks on Iran and Lebanon seem to confirm this

stories.title: Mégsem nyújtják be jövő héten a Nemzeti Vagyonvisszaszerzési és Védelmi Hivatalról szóló javaslatot
stories.summary: Magyar Péter azt mondta, előtte társadalmi egyeztetésre bocsátják a törvénytervezetet, de reméli, hogy szeptember elején fel tud állni a hivatal.

stories.title: Nem hagyhatja el az országot a spanyol miniszterelnök korrupcióval vádolt felesége
stories.summary: Begoña Gómeznek az útlevelét is le kellett adnia, a tárgyalás időpontját még nem tűzték ki.

stories.title: Hegedűs Zsolt a tatabányai kisfiú haláláról: Nem engedhetjük meg magunknak, hogy ezekből az esetekből ne tanuljunk
stories.summary: Szerinte az egészségügyben Magyarországon sokszor nem a tanulás lett a reflex, hanem a szőnyeg alá söprés és az egyének hibáztatása.

stories.title: Nyolc évre ítélték a tömegmészárlással fenyegetőző, Magyarországon élő norvég férfit
stories.summary: A férfi korábban arról is beszélt, hogy túl akarja szárnyalni a norvég tömeggyilkos, Anders Breivik mészárlását, aki 77 embert ölt meg 2011-ben.

stories.title: Egy játék mind felett: kipróbáltuk az eddigi legjobb társasjáték-adaptációt Tolkien világából
stories.summary: Két játékot is teszteltünk, ami A Gyűrűk Urára épül, az egyik a legapróbb rezdüléseiben is követi az eredeti sztorit, a másik nagy szabadságot ad az újrajátszásában, de a maga nemében mindkettő nagy tisztelettel nyúl a klasszikushoz.

stories.title: Ismét Európa-bajnok a magyar férfi kardcsapat
stories.summary: A Szilágyi Áron, Szatmári András, Rabb Krisztián, Dallos Benedek összeállítású csapat 45-43-ra győzte le a románokat a döntőben.

stories.title: Észrevétlenül terjedhet az ebola a kongói menekülttáborokban
stories.summary: A 15 ezer főt elszállásoló Kigonze táborban május eleje óta már 30 ember halt meg, mindegyikük ebolagyanús tüneteket mutatott.

stories.title: Czech government causes alarm with plan to scrap license fee
stories.summary: The Czech government has approved a bill to put the funding of public service media under direct political control. Critics warn that if passed, the bill would put a pillar of Czech democracy at risk.

stories.title: Poland: Arrest after Russian artist and Putin critic killed
stories.summary: Prime Minister Donald Tusk says Lublin police arrested a suspect in the fatal shooting of the Russian national and critical artist known as Semyon Skrepetsky. He said investigators were still seeking a "mastermind."

stories.title: CDC to tap $107m in emergency funding for Ebola response in DRC and Uganda
stories.summary: Number of people infected now tops 1,000 though health officials say the global risk remains low Sign up for the Breaking News US newsletter email The Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) will tap $107m in emergency funding for Ebola outbreak response in the Democratic Republic of the Congo ( DRC ) and Uganda , officials said on Thursday. The continued Ebola outbreak in the DRC comes as Canada, Mexico and the US jointly host the Fifa World Cup , attracting visitors from around the wo

stories.title: Russian dissident artist Semyon Skrepezki murdered in Poland
stories.summary: Skrepezki was known for his scathing caricatures of Russian President Vladimir Putin. His suspected killer has been apprehended.

stories.title: 'Warning fire went up': Couple on board yacht describe encounter with Russian warship
stories.summary: The Russian frigate fired warning shots near a British retired couple on a yacht in the English Channel.

stories.title: Ukraine war briefing: Drones strike Russia’s Tyumen oil refinery 2,000km away, says Zelenskyy
stories.summary: Reports from Siberia confirm attack, while Ukrainian president says new weapon has 3,000km range; occupied Crimea under attack. What we know on day 1,579 Volodymyr Zelenskiy has confirmed that Ukrainian drones attacked an oil refinery in Russia’s Tyumen region ⁠in western Siberia, ⁠ more ​than 2,000 km (1,200 miles) from Ukraine . He said Ukrainian company Fire Point had developed new long-range drones capable of ⁠travelling more than 3,000km and they had been “successfully deployed”. In his nig

stories.title: Middle East live: US-Iran talks to begin in Switzerland as Tehran says it has closed Strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: Iran says decision comes after waves of Israeli strikes in Lebanon, as delegations arrive as Swiss resort for talks Iran says it is closing strait of Hormuz over Israeli strikes in Lebanon Iran has said it is closing the strait of Hormuz after waves of Israeli strikes in Lebanon in a move that threatens to derail the fragile interim peace deal with the US, signed just days ago. The Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps warned ships not to approach the strategic waterway, which before the war carried

stories.title: Vance arrives in Switzerland for Iran peace talks with Hormuz in spotlight
stories.summary: U.S. Vice President JD Vance arrived in Switzerland on Sunday for high-stakes peace talks with Iran, saying before takeoff that Washington had “a couple days of talks” to try to launch the next phase of President Donald Trump’s fragile ceasefire agreement. Vance and Second Lady Usha Vance landed at Emmen Air Base outside Lucerne shortly before 6 a.m. local time, according to his office.  He joins U.S. special envoy Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner, Trump’s son-in-law, at the Bürgenstock reso

stories.title: Bolivian president declares state of emergency and deploys military to quell anti-government protests
stories.summary: Bulldozers sent in to clear roadblocks that have stifled the country as farmers and Indigenous groups protest against conservative president Bolivia’s president declared a state of emergency on Saturday and deployed soldiers and bulldozers to raze anti-government roadblocks that have paralysed the country. For more than six weeks, unions, Indigenous groups and coca farmers have marched through cities and blocked roads across the country with rubble, logs and debris in protest against the conserv

stories.title: Iran says it is closing strait of Hormuz over Israeli strikes in Lebanon
stories.summary: Unclear if threat has been carried out or if move will jeopardise talks with US scheduled for Sunday Middle East latest updates Iran has said it is closing the strait of Hormuz after waves of Israeli strikes in Lebanon in a move that threatens to derail the fragile interim peace deal with the US, signed just days ago. The Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps warned ships not to approach the strategic waterway, which before the war carried a fifth of global oil and liquid gas supplies, citing what i

stories.title: ‘There’s no jobs’: struggle and regret in a Welsh town that backed Brexit
stories.summary: Ten years ago Ebbw Vale had the highest proportion of leave voters in Wales despite huge EU funding, which has not been fully replaced Where Ebbw Vale’s steelworks once stood is now a cluster of gleaming modern buildings including a hospital, a leisure centre and a college. Over the past decade, these public facilities have been joined by a public-private cybersecurity research centre and two tech firms. A new railway station opened at the site in 2015. Yet, during the Guardian’s visit to the We

stories.title: Russian strikes on eastern Ukraine kill three and injure 22
stories.summary: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said on Saturday that his forces had targeted an oil-processing facility in Russia's Tyumen region.

stories.title: Revealed: Brexit voting areas have seen faster growth in foreign workers since EU referendum
stories.summary: Guardian investigation also finds same areas experienced relative decline over same period Leave-voting areas have seen faster relative growth in foreign workers since the Brexit referendum, a Guardian investigation has found. Data analysis suggests that the decade since the Brexit vote may not have matched the expectations of many Leave supporters, showing their local areas have also become relatively more deprived over the same period. Continue reading...

stories.title: Israel continues strikes in Lebanon despite ceasefire with Hezbollah
stories.summary: Israeli strikes in southern Lebanon killed several people amid overnight exchanges of fire between Israeli forces and Iran-backed Hezbollah. According to the Lebanese Civil Defense , 16 people were killed and 12 wounded in Israeli attacks on the city of Nabatieh and nearby villages in southern Lebanon. The Israeli military confirmed strikes against what it described as “ Hezbollah terrorist targets in southern Lebanon” after Hezbollah had “launched more than 50 projectiles at Israeli forces,” th

stories.title: Iran announces plans to close Strait of Hormuz, alleging ‘violations of the ceasefire’
stories.summary: Iran is again planning to close the Strait of Hormuz, according to an Iranian consulate and reports from Iranian state-owned media on Saturday, throwing yet another wrench into U.S. President Donald Trump’s plans to sell his peace agreement with Tehran to a skeptical electorate . Iran’s Consulate-General in India wrote on X on Saturday that the closure will be partly “in response to the continuous violations of the ceasefire in southern Lebanon” and will come as the “first step as a response to

stories.title: Colombia’s runoff election expected to trigger shift in decades-long armed conflict
stories.summary: Frontrunner Abelardo de la Espriella has vowed to return to full-scale military confrontation with armed groups Colombians go to the polls on Sunday in a presidential runoff expected to trigger to a dramatic shift in the country’s decades-long armed conflict, now at its most violent point since the landmark 2016 peace agreement between the government and most of the Revolutionary Armed Forces of Colombia (Farc). Polls show the frontrunner is the Trump-admiring far-right lawyer and millionaire bu

stories.title: Canada’s policies force asylum seekers into US to face deportation, critics say
stories.summary: Advocates say the Safe Third Country Agreement forces immigrants to head to an unsafe country: the United States It was the threat of gang violence in Honduras that pushed Carlos and Antonia to flee their home. In 2021, with their toddler, Alejandro, and a handful of belongings, the married couple ventured north hoping to reach safety in the US. The journey, through Guatemala and Mexico , was filled with danger and uncertainty Continue reading...

stories.title: Sweat, tears and camaraderie as 20,000 runners take on world’s largest ultramarathon
stories.summary: For one day every June, South Africa’s searing racial inequality seems to melt away at Comrades race In the early morning dark, thousands of runners waited, jostling with anticipation. South Africa’s national anthem rang out. Then the haunting swell of Shosholoza , first sung by Zimbabwean migrant workers in South Africa’s goldmines. Finally, that unmistakable, spine-tingling piano: Chariots of Fire. Runners gather before the start of the marathon Continue reading...

stories.title: ‘They have all the power’: investigation finds that 93% of ICE arrests targeted Latinos
stories.summary: 430 ICE street arrests filed over a five-month period were clustered in predominantly Latino communities across New Jersey and New York Federal agents have arrested hundreds of immigrants off New York and New Jersey streets in recent months in a stealth enforcement campaign that disproportionately targeted people from Latin American countries, according to an investigation by the City Reporter based on a review of more than 1,200 lawsuits. More than 93% of the people grabbed off area streets who

stories.title: Trump loyalist Jim Jordan linked to group that received ‘dark money’ from ICE detention contractor
stories.summary: Report finds close ties between the Trump administration and Geo Group, which profits from anti-immigration crackdown Jim Jordan is among the most famous names in this stretch of Ohio. The congressman and chair of the powerful House judiciary committee is considered among the most conservative and influential members in Congress, and is a longtime loyalist of Donald Trump. Continue reading...

stories.title: Ukraine hits Russia-controlled Crimea in deadly drone attack
stories.summary: Footage of fires at a major depot on Crimea circulated on social media. The Moscow-aligned governor of Crimea has halted public fuel sales after the attack.

stories.title: Israeli strikes kill six people in Gaza including Al Jazeera cameraman, officials say
stories.summary: The Israeli military accused Ahmed Wishah of being a "Hamas sniper operative", without providing evidence.

stories.title: Colombia's escalating, brutal internal conflict is defining its presidential election
stories.summary: A left-wing senator who backs talks with armed gangs faces an outsider endorsed by Trump.

stories.title: US and Iranian delegations arrive in Switzerland for peace deal talks
stories.summary: "We're going to hopefully make progress on the nuclear issue, make progress on the Lebanon ceasefire issue," US Vice President JD Vance told reporters.

stories.title: Meloni, Trump trade barbs in escalating spat after G7
stories.summary: U.S. President Donald Trump on Saturday stuck hard to his claim that Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni “begged” for a photo with him at the G7 summit earlier this week, drawing a sharp retort from the Italian leader. “These constant, unprovoked attacks are senseless,” Meloni said after Trump’s latest broadside against her. The two right-wing leaders, once politically close , have been at loggerheads since Trump on Friday told Italian television channel La7 that

stories.title: Sánchez’s wife barred from leaving Spain ahead of corruption trial
stories.summary: Spanish Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez’s wife, Begoña Gómez, has been barred from leaving Spain and will be required to appear in court twice a month under a judge’s decision issued on Saturday. The measures were imposed as part of proceedings ahead of a corruption trial against Gómez and are intended to prevent any risk of flight before a verdict is reached. The case — which also names Gómez’s adviser Cristina Álvarez and businessman Juan Carlos Barrabés as defendants — involves

stories.title: How Europe’s EV makers shrank their product to challenge the bloated SUVs
stories.summary: Smaller, cheaper cars built for narrow city streets are becoming more stylish – but require careful design decisions The winding backstreets of London, Paris and Rome are a large part of their charm. But they are also a problem for electric carmakers. For a long time, squeezing big batteries into smaller, cheaper cars to fit European streets was too much of a problem, so manufacturers focused on bloated SUVs instead. But that is finally changing. Battery technology has improved and Europe’s carm

stories.title: Angus Taylor dismisses Labor’s ‘half-arsed’ tweaks to CGT reform – as it happened
stories.summary: This blog has now closed Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Ted O’Brien distanced himself from Pauline Hanson ’s suggestion that Australia shouldn’t give aid to Pacific countries that also take aid from China. He said it was a legitimate concern, but her solution was “completely wrong” for the Pacific and not in Australia’s national interest. The idea that you effectively hold a gun to the head of our Pacific neighbours – that’s not what a friend does, that’s not a way

stories.title: Two-thirds of EU citizens back UK rejoining bloc, survey finds
stories.summary: Poll also finds three quarters of people in Britain want closer ties, with majority accepting free movement Two-thirds of EU citizens would back Britain rejoining the bloc, while most UK voters say Brexit has been bad for the issues they care about and want closer ties, including levels of integration – such as free movement – long seen as toxic, a survey has found. Ten years after the Brexit referendum, the polling by the European Council on Foreign Relations (ECFR) , a thinktank, found 66% of

stories.title: Suppliers unable to chase fees after film producer’s 50 companies are struck off
stories.summary: Removal of Alan Latham’s firms means there is no longer an entity for creditors to make claims against A prolific film producer, whose projects have starred Frasier’s Kelsey Grammer and Four Weddings and a Funeral’s Anna Chancellor , has had scores of his production businesses forcibly removed from the UK’s companies register, leaving workers unable to chase unpaid fees. Alan Latham, whose low-budget films have previously raised questions over his use of tax credits , has seen 50 of his film bus

stories.title: ‘Build Vice City’: the GTA 6 scam that’s hitting gamers worldwide
stories.summary: Bank details at risk as criminals use AI to create fake sites and emails offering pre-release beta test version Like millions of gamers around the world, you have been waiting years for Grand Theft Auto VI to be released. Now you have the opportunity to play the much-anticipated game before everyone else. An email has arrived inviting you to play a pre-release “beta” version of the game so that you can alert the makers to any bugs before its official release later this year. Continue reading...

stories.title: An Armenian tycoon has a private zoo. Now he wants the world’s biggest Jesus statue
stories.summary: Gagik Tsarukyan hopes project will resonate with global movement that blends religious faith, nationalism and cultural conservatism Behind the walls of a sprawling estate on the outskirts of Yerevan, six tigers prowl behind a fence, three lions pace their enclosures, and alligators bask in the afternoon heat. Further into the compound, more animals appear. Beneath a gilded, hand-painted ceiling, a dining hall houses a taxidermy menagerie: white tigers reared on their hind legs, a stuffed eagle p

stories.title: Keir Starmer expected to announce departure as prime minister on Monday
stories.summary: Business secretary says Starmer is reflecting on ‘political realities’ amid overwhelming pressure from MPs UK politics – live updates Keir Starmer is expected to announce on Monday that he will step down as prime minister, after overwhelming pressure from Labour MPs to make way for Andy Burnham to become Labour leader. Speaking for the government on Sunday, Peter Kyle, the business secretary, refused to comment on Starmer’s specific plans but said the prime minister was aware of the “political r

stories.title: Starmer expected to announce departure on Monday as growing numbers of MPs back Burnham for PM – UK politics live
stories.summary: Business secretary says PM spending the weekend ‘making time to reflect on the political realities’ he faces Keir Starmer expected to announce departure as prime minister on Monday Some commentators have said Andy Burnham is just a better communicator than Keir Starmer, but have questioned how different he is on policy. The Guardian’s policy editor, Kiran Stacey, has helpfully looked at the political projects a Burnham government would likely pursue in this useful explainer: Continue reading...

stories.title: US treasury chief urged Trump not to host ‘Mr Bean on crack’ Zelenskyy, book says
stories.summary: Suggestion that Scott Bessent so described a world leader included in Regime Change, by New York Times reporters Scott Bessent, the US treasury secretary, advised Donald Trump not to host Volodymyr Zelenskyy in the Oval Office, having called the Ukrainian president a “little fucker”, a “special-needs child” and “Mr Bean on crack”, according to a new book. The suggestion that a US cabinet official described a world leader in such terms is included in Regime Change, a blockbusting account of the s

stories.title: Al Jazeera cameraman Ahmed Wishah killed in Israeli strike on Gaza
stories.summary: Wishah among at least 260 journalists killed since Israel’s war on Gaza began in October 2023 Qatar-based news network Al Jazeera has said one of its journalists was killed by an Israeli strike in Gaza on Saturday, becoming one of the at least 260 Palestinian journalists to have been killed since Israel’s war on Gaza began in October 2023. Ahmed Wishah, a cameraman for the network, was killed in a strike targeting a house in the Bureij refugee camp in central Gaza, the broadcaster said on its we

stories.title: LAPD releases footage of moment officers shot and killed woman’s dog
stories.summary: After Jameson the dog, wearing a blue Knicks jersey, walked out into the hallway, an officer fired his pistol four times The Los Angeles police department has released footage of the moment when officers shot and killed a woman’s dog in the hallway outside her apartment in the Canoga Park neighborhood. Police had responded to reports of a woman screaming on 13 June, which turned out to be cheering, the night that the New York Knicks defeated the San Antonio Spurs to win the NBA finals. Continue

stories.title: Undav heroics help Germany beat Ivory Coast
stories.summary: In Germany's second 2026 World Cup game, Deniz Undav helped Germany to a comeback win against Ivory Coast in Toronto.

stories.title: US and Iranian negotiators head to Switzerland for talks
stories.summary: US Vice President JD Vance has arrived in Switzerland for talks with Iran's delegation in the mountain resort in Bürgenstock, Switzerland. Follow DW.

stories.title: Sudan: UN warns of mass atrocities in El-Obeid
stories.summary: The United Nations Security Council has called on the paramilitary RSF in Sudan to not launch an attack on the city of El-Obeid.

stories.title: Germany could scrap law banning insults against politicians
stories.summary: Germany's special law prohibiting the insulting of politicians has led to cases that many people consider absurd. Some members of the government want to abolish the law; others think there's a good reason to retain it.

stories.title: India news: Millions of students retake NEET amid protests
stories.summary: Some 2.2 million premedical aspirants are retaking the high-pressure entrance test across India under strict security measures. Meanwhile, Prime Minister Modi is in Kolkata for International Yoga Day. DW has more.

stories.title: Spain: PM's wife must face corruption trial, judge rules
stories.summary: A court in Madrid ruled that there is sufficient evidence against Begona Gomez, the wife of Prime Minister Pedro Sanchez. The 55-year-old university director is banned from leaving the country.

stories.title: Why an AI company cleaned my New York City apartment for free
stories.summary: An AI company is sending free cleaners door-to-door in a bid to train the robots it hopes one day will replace them.

stories.title: At CrimeCon true crime obsessives come face-to-face with real loss
stories.summary: Victims' families say there are ways to be an ethical true-crime fan - not an exploitative one.

stories.title: Backstage at Gorillaz' epic, one-off stadium show: 'The vibe is ridiculous'
stories.summary: Damon Albarn, De La Soul and Moonchild Sannelly talk backstage as Gorillaz play their biggest show.

stories.title: US-Iran talks to begin in Switzerland as Tehran says it closed Strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: The US disputed Iran's claim the waterway is shut, a move Tehran says was a response to Israeli attacks in Lebanon.

stories.title: Former Olympian denies vandalising Washington Reflecting Pool after arrest
stories.summary: Davey Hearn says he was simply touching the new paint at the site out of curiosity and did not remove or alter it.

stories.title: Zelenskyy issues one-week ultimatum to Lukashenka over drone-guidance equipment
stories.summary: During a media briefing in Kyiv on Friday, Zelenskyy threatened Ukraine will take down the drone relay equipment on the border if the Belarusian leader, Aliaksandr Lukashenka, doesn't do anything to address it.

stories.title: Trump threatens Hormuz tolls as ships sail on despite IRGC's closure claim
stories.summary: Iran's IRGC said it had closed the Strait of Hormuz over Israeli strikes on Lebanon, but CENTCOM said 55 ships carrying 17 million barrels of oil transited unhindered. Trump meanwhile warned of US tolls if a final deal was not reached within 60 days.

stories.title: Bolivian president declares state of emergency
stories.summary: The move comes after weeks of anti-government protests that have caused a shortage of basic goods in Bolivia.

stories.title: Lebanon air raids kill 18 as residents flee southern villages
stories.summary: Lebanon reported 18 deaths on 19 June after Israeli airstrikes hit several locations in the country's south and east, raising fresh concerns about the durability of a regional peace initiative announced earlier this week.

stories.title: What lessons will Iran’s new leadership draw from the 110-day war?
stories.summary: Now fighting is over, the question is how Iran’s government will behave. Early clues point to more authoritarianism and prioritising relations with China The precise ideological lessons that Iran’s new leadership draws from the 110-day war may prove to be the overriding factor in determining whether negotiations with the US culminate in an agreement that verifiably prevents the country from developing a nuclear weapon – an outcome that could usher in a new era for the Iranian economy while also

stories.title: Israeli attack kills famed turtle sanctuary ecologist in Lebanon

stories.summary: A huge fire tore through a four-star beach resort in the Dominican Republic, killing one woman. About 1,700 tourists were evacuated as the fire appeared to destroy most of the hotel.

stories.title: Inequality is elementary to Germany's school system

stories.title: South African men sentenced in ‘world’s largest’ rhino horn trafficking case
stories.summary: ‘Mastermind’ Dawie Groenewald given fine of 2m rand or four-year jail term almost 16 years after arrest Two traffickers of rhino horns have been sentenced by a South African court in what police said was the world’s largest such case, partly bringing to an end an almost two-decade legal saga. Dawie Groenewald and Tielman Erasmus had faced more than 1,700 charges ranging from illegally hunting and dehorning rhinos to racketeering and money laundering. Continue reading...

stories.title: ‘Cynical to get power’: Michel Barnier on Boris Johnson, Brexit and the EU’s future
stories.summary: Former negotiator believes in an unstable world, it is ‘perfectly possible’ the UK can rejoin the EU with old opt-outs UK could keep special pre-Brexit terms if it rejoined EU, Michel Barnier says A couple of years ago, Michel Barnier spent a weekend with Boris Johnson’s father, Stanley. It was not some ghoulish Brexit spin-off of The Traitors, but the result of the former EU negotiator’s wife, Isabelle, being a close friend of Johnson’s French cousin, Anne du Boucheron, the owner of Château de

stories.title: Weather tracker: Severe thunderstorms sweep Europe and east Asia
stories.summary: Strong winds and heavy rain batter Slovenia, while France experiences atypical heatwave Severe thunderstorms swept across the Balkans last week, bringing widespread destruction to parts of the region. The storms developed as unstable hot air lingered over the Adriatic Sea while a cold front plunged south-eastward. The front began its journey on 10 June in Slovenia, where the Slovenian Environment Agency recorded 65mph gusts at Ljubljana airport. Heavy rain also fell widely across the region with

stories.title: Taliban order ban on smartphones as officials shown destroying devices
stories.summary: Directive aimed at government workers, but reports of wider implementation spark warnings of future Afghanistan-wide prohibition The Taliban have ordered a sweeping ban on the use of smartphones by government officials – in what some analysts say could foreshadow broader, population-level restrictions. In a directive issued by the Taliban’s military courts and reviewed by the Guardian, the ban was to take effect this week and prohibits “high rank, low rank, general mujahideen, or service staff”

stories.title: Berlin summit brings out private equity protesters
stories.summary: Activists gathered to protest the SuperReturn private equity summit in Berlin, saying the industry drives inequality, job cuts and rising costs as investors representing trillions of Euros gather to seek high returns.

stories.title: How a feel-good German song is taking over social media
stories.summary: Being encouraging and building confidence is what the German song "Gut genug" ("Good Enough") is all about. A meme is now bringing the track and its message to millions of people around the world.

stories.title: Strait of Hormuz: German military prepares for possible deployment
stories.summary: Following the tentative deal with the US, Iran is planning to reopen the Strait of Hormuz. The German Bundeswehr is planning to assist with the mine-clearing.

stories.title: Germany's 2026 World Cup squad built on African roots
stories.summary: Germany's 2026 World Cup squad has leaders with African heritage. The diversity of the team is a strength.

stories.title: US curbs Anthropic AI access, raising global concerns
stories.summary: The Trump administration has restricted foreign access to Anthropic's latest AI models over security fears, forcing a global shutdown. Could the row hurt the Claude-maker's IPO plans and set a far-reaching precedent?

stories.title: Israel says one soldier killed and several injured in clashes following latest strikes in Lebanon
stories.summary: Israeli officials close to Netanyahu say the Likud leader has no intention to give up positions in Lebanon and are engaged in negotiations with the US following the announcement of an initial deal with Iran to halt hostilities.

stories.title: US and Iran sign deal, but who really won? Here's what to know
stories.summary: Trump signed the framework deal at Versailles while Pezeshkian signed in Tehran, pledging to reopen the Strait of Hormuz and triggering 60 days of further talks. Analysts say the terms leave Tehran in a stronger position than the deal's framing suggests.

stories.title: Will Putin change tactics after Ukrainian drone attacks?
stories.summary: Steve Rosenberg explains what Russia's next steps may be after frequent Ukrainian drone attacks.

stories.title: Toronto celebrates as Ghana beat Panama with late World Cup goal
stories.summary: Canada became the backdrop for emotional celebrations as Ghana secured a late World Cup victory over Panama in Toronto. Supporters from both nations turned the city into a vibrant meeting point before the decisive Group L match.

stories.title: Weapons, money and ships: How is this Iran deal different from others?
stories.summary: BBC Verify examines how the new deal between the US and Iran affects three key areas.

stories.title: How do Iranians and Israelis view the US-Iran deal?
stories.summary: Residents in Tehran and Tel Aviv voiced mixed reactions to the US-Iran agreement, with some welcoming an end to the fighting while others questioned whether the deal would hold.

stories.title: Should the US impose a teen social media ban like the UK?
stories.summary: The UK is enforcing a social media ban for children under the age of 16. The BBC asked Americans of different ages if they thought it was a good idea.

stories.title: Germany and Uzbekistan deepen ties, President Steinmeier tells Euronews
stories.summary: Trade between Germany and Uzbekistan exceeded $1.4 billion (€1.2bn) last year, while German investment reached $2 billion (€1.7bn). The two countries are also expanding educational exchanges, labour mobility programmes and business partnerships.

stories.title: Lebanon: Tyre residents return to homes shattered by strikes
stories.summary: Lebanese families returning to Tyre found their homes in ruins on 18 June after Israeli strikes and voiced cautious optimism about a new US-Iran agreement.

stories.title: Iran's ayatollah says he approved US framework deal despite having 'different view'
stories.summary: Meanwhile, US Vice President JD Vance defended the deal, saying he would likely head to Switzerland for talks to turn it into a long-term agreement.

stories.title: Macron and Modi tour Indian pavilion at France's VivaTech
stories.summary: France highlighted its technology partnership with India on 18 June as President Emmanuel Macron and Prime Minister Narendra Modi toured VivaTech 2026 in Paris.

stories.title: Knicks revel in NBA victory parade as thousands of fans fill New York's streets
stories.summary: New York erupted in jubilant scenes on Saturday when the Knicks broke a 53-year drought to defeat the San Antonio Spurs away in Texas.
